nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical marine environments; typically anchored in sandy or rubble substrates near reef flats, sheltered lagoons, and coastal shallows

decorPreferences: sandy substrate for anchoring (minimum 5 cm sand bed)

imageCandidates.notes: Primary Mosaic Macros product image for Ringed Finger (Neomeris annulata)

summary: Ringed Finger is a lightly calcified Caribbean green macroalgae in the family Dasycladaceae, forming upright, finger-like growths with distinctive concentric banding. Rated advanced; has a reputation for spreading aggressively once introduced.
careNotes:
Ringed Finger (Neomeris annulata) is a lightly calcified green macroalgae in the family Dasycladaceae, the same family as the Fern Finger ([sw-algae-036]) and Jointed Tuft ([sw-algae-055]). It produces erect, cylindrical finger-like thalli with distinctive concentric banding — the source of both the species epithet (annulata, meaning 'ringed') and its common name. The thallus is lightly to moderately calcified with calcium carbonate deposits in concentric zones.

INVASIVENESS WARNING: Neomeris annulata has been reported as very invasive and very difficult to remove once introduced to a reef system. It tends to spread from its initial placement and may be difficult to remove from rockwork or substrate. This should be considered carefully before introduction to a display tank.

As a calcareous green alga in the Dasycladales, stable calcium (400–450 ppm) and alkalinity (kH 8–12) are generally regarded as important for maintaining healthy calcification. Fluctuations in these parameters have been associated with tissue bleaching or decline in related species. High lighting is generally recommended, consistent with its shallow-water Caribbean habitat. Growth rate is typically described as slow — calcareous deposition is energetically costly — though once established this species may spread actively via new thalli from the holdfast.

This species is sand-anchored in nature and tends to be sold on rock with multiple individual growths. Providing a sand bed of at least 5 cm is generally advisable to allow natural anchoring; it can also be wedged into rockwork. Recommended water parameters for this species are: temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm, nitrate 5–20 ppm, and phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and other trace elements is generally recommended for calcareous macroalgae. Full-spectrum LED lighting is advised; actinic-only spectra are generally insufficient for demanding species like this. Flow should be gentle to moderate — excessive flow may tear or dislodge growths.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole aquarium hobby source for this species. Product description confirms lightly calcified upright thalli, sold on rock with 5+ growths, and includes invasiveness warning.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — General Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source providing canonical water parameter targets (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm), lighting, flow, and sand-bed acceptance guidance. Meets 2-source minimum for Mosaic-only entries per playbook.
